message:
fix(analysis): cap k-paths DFS enumeration and include missing config flags in cache key

advanced.rs: The compute_k_paths DFS could enumerate an unbounded number
of paths before the top-k selection, causing O(exponential) memory and
CPU on dense graphs. Add a path_cap of min(10*k, 10_000) and break out
of the outer source loop once the cap is reached. This bounds the
worst-case enumeration while still providing enough candidate paths for
meaningful top-k selection.

cache.rs: Three AnalysisConfig flags — enable_critical_path,
enable_articulation, and enable_slack — were not mixed into the cache
key hash. This meant toggling any of those flags would incorrectly
return a cached result computed with different settings. Add all three
flags to the blake3 hash input using the same bi-state prefix pattern
(cp:/ap:/sl:) as the existing flags.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
